caveat: All figures are contested, lag events on the ground, and mix belligerent claims with independent estimates. Treat every number as an order of magnitude, not a fact.
background: The 2026 Iran war began on 28 February when the United States and Israel launched Operation Epic Fury — coordinated strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in the opening hours. Iran retaliated against Israel, US bases, and Gulf states; Hezbollah opened a Lebanon front days later. A memorandum signed 17 June at Versailles and Tehran collapsed on 8 July, and fighting has since re-centered on control of the Strait of Hormuz, which the IRGC declared closed on 12 July.

status.headline: War 'teeters on all-out' — another US death, ninth night of strikes 'in honour' of the fallen, oil tops $90
status.detail: The renewed war is sliding toward the all-out fight both governments said they wanted to avoid. A US service member was killed and another wounded in Iraq during a 'controlled detonation' of a downed Iranian drone — a second theatre of American losses that brings the war's US death toll to 17 (per ABC News), two days after two troops were killed and one left missing at Jordan's Muwaffaq Salti Airbase. President Trump said a ninth consecutive night of US strikes was carried out 'in honour' of the fallen soldiers, recasting the campaign as open reprisal; the Washington Post reported the US now 'teeters on all-out war' and CNN warned it is 'threatening to spin out of control.' Crude passed $90 a barrel as Iran vowed 'not a single drop' of oil or gas would pass the Strait of Hormuz while strikes continue, and Israel warned Iran's attacks are 'coming close' to its territory, restating its 'full force' threat. Tehran has called off the interim agreement and IAEA access stays suspended. Cutting the other way, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio signaled openness to diplomacy; UN chief António Guterres continues to urge renewed talks.

stats.label: Killed in Iran (Iranian figures)
stats.note: US/Israeli estimates put Iranian military deaths alone above 6,000

stats.label: US service members killed
stats.note: Per an ABC News tally, 19 July, in a war fought largely in the air; includes the two killed and one missing at Jordan's Muwaffaq Salti Airbase (per CENTCOM) and one killed in Iraq during a 'controlled detonation' of a downed Iranian drone, 19 July; 548+ wounded

stats.label: Left without water in southern Iran
stats.note: Iranian figure after US strikes on water infrastructure, 18 July

stats.label: Iranian naval vessels destroyed or damaged
Global oil supply disruption
stats.note: Hormuz carries roughly a fifth of global oil flows

stats.label: Crude oil price
stats.note: Passed $90 a barrel on 20 July — its highest of the war — as Iran vowed 'not a single drop' would pass Hormuz; US diesel has topped $5 a gallon

stats.label: Strait of Hormuz status
stats.note: Declared closed by the IRGC on 12 July; US enforcing counter-blockade; Iran vows 'not a single drop' of oil or gas will pass while strikes continue

stats.label: IAEA safeguards access
stats.note: Iran declared normal safeguards 'legally untenable' after strikes on Natanz; on 18 July it also suspended its commitments to the interim deal with the US

stats.label: Bushehr nuclear power plant
stats.note: Satellite images show fresh damage from strikes dated 7–12 July; Bushehr is Iran's largest nuclear plant

actors.members: United States, Israel; Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain after Iranian strikes; support from UK, Jordan, Qatar, Turkey and others
Iran (Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, President Pezeshkian), Hezbollah, Houthis, Popular Mobilization Forces, Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad
